title: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

TO: Freedom of Information Officer

Village of La Grange

53 S. La Grange Road

La Grange, IL 60525

DATE: March 11, 2026

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request for public records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting the following records regarding Plan Commission Cases 284, 285, 286, 287, 288, and 289:

Reimbursable Charge Records: All documents identifying or listing reimbursable charges, actual costs, or fees incurred by the Village in processing these specific applications.

Invoices: All complete invoices (including those from professional and technical consultants, legal counsel, court reporters, or secretarial services) associated with these cases.

Correspondence: All correspondence regarding the assessment, billing, or collection of these charges between Village staff, the applicants, the property owners, or third-party consultants.

Request for Fee Waiver (Public Interest)
Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6(c), I am requesting a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is in the public interest because:

Dissemination to the Public: The principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the fiscal management and legal transparency of the Village’s Plan Commission proceedings to the general public.

Non-Commercial: This request is not for personal or commercial benefit.

Public Welfare: Understanding how public resources and reimbursable expenses are managed directly impacts the welfare and legal rights of the residents of La Grange.

Instructions for Response
Format: Please provide these records in an electronic format (PDF/Excel) to the email address provided below.

Statutory Deadlines: As a reminder, under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), if the Village fails to respond within the statutory timeframe, it forfeits the right to charge fees and may not treat this request as "unduly burdensome" under Section 3(g).

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption for each redaction and provide the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

Thank you,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Plan Commission Reimbursable Charges
communications.communication:
Subject: DEMAND FOR IMMEDIATE PRODUCTION: Notice of FOIA Violations (Request Submitted March 11, 2026)

To: FOIA Officer, Village of La Grange
From: Concerned Citizen of La Grange
Date: March 29, 2026

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing regarding the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request I submitted to the Village of La Grange on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 11:34 PM.

On Friday, March 27, 2026, the Village provided a partial production of records while withholding a large portion of the requested documents under the claim that fulfilling the request would be "unduly burdensome."

Please be advised that the Village’s use of the "unduly burdensome" exemption is legally invalid due to multiple procedural violations of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). The Village is now in a state of "deemed denial" and has forfeited its right to claim this exemption.

1. Untimely Extension and Forfeiture of Exemption (5 ILCS 140/3(d))
My request was received on Thursday, March 12. The statutory five-business-day deadline to respond or request an extension was the close of business (5:00 PM) on Thursday, March 19. The Village’s email attempting to invoke a five-day extension was not sent until 5:52 PM on March 19, well after business hours. Under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), a public body that fails to respond within the statutory timeframe "may not treat the request as unduly burdensome under subsection (g)." By missing the initial deadline, the Village legally forfeited the right to use this exemption.

2. Missed Extension Deadline
Even if the after-hours extension request had been valid, the new deadline for production would have been Thursday, March 26. The Village failed to produce any records until Friday, March 27, further cementing the request’s status as a deemed denial.

3. Failure to "Meet and Confer" (5 ILCS 140/3(g))
Furthermore, even if the Village had responded on time, it would have violated the mandatory procedural requirements of Section 3(g). The statute explicitly mandates that "before invoking this exemption, the public body shall extend to the person making the request an opportunity to confer with it in an attempt to reduce the request to manageable proportions." The Village issued a partial denial without ever offering this mandatory opportunity to confer.

Demand for Production
Because the Village failed to comply with the statutory deadlines set forth in 5 ILCS 140/3, the claim that the request is unduly burdensome is void. Furthermore, pursuant to Section 3(d), the Village has waived its right to charge any reproduction fees for this request.

I demand the immediate production of the remaining withheld records. If the Village does not provide the remaining documents by the close of business on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, I will file a formal Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General to compel production and report these procedural violations and/or explore filing a lawsuit with an attorney.

I look forward to your prompt compliance.

This request was to review the situation to consider a news article on the billing based on the concerns brought up in a public comment session, so understanding why one applicant was charged so differently from the others could be greatly in the public interest, pending the facts of the situation. Someone making the situation known in the public forum surely has pertinent communication with the village about the situation, and hopefully, an extensive explanation from the city staff.  

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

title: Retreat Expense Reports from BOARD of ED trustees

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Retreat Expense Reports from BOARD of ED trustees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

a) All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b) Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mary Lee

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #164667-00782640
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following New York Freedom of Information Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 5, 2024. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Aug. 8, 2024. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.  You had assigned it reference number #164667-00782640.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115
Attn: Mary Lee
	RE: 	FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024
Dear Ms. Lee:
	As you are aware, the East Williston Union Free School District (“District”) received your Freedom of Information Law (“FOIL”) request dated June 5, 2024.  Specifically, you requested the following: 
a)	All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b)	Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
	Please be advised the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.
	In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As such, it is anticipated that the District will either grant or deny your request on or before September 6, 2024. 
Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in-whole or in-part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr. Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road, Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).
									Sincerely, 
								
									DIANE CASTONGUAY
									Records Access Officer

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Retreat Expense Reports from BOARD of ED trustees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following New York Freedom of Information Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on June 5, 2024. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Feb. 4, 2025. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

February 4, 2025
RE: 164667-00782640

MuckRock News
DEPT MR 164667
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

Attn: Mary Lee

	RE: 	Partial Determination to FOIL Request dated June 5, 2024

Dear Ms. Lee:

This letter serves as a partial determination concerning your email request dated June 5, 2024 for records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Law (“FOIL”).  Specifically, you requested the following: 
a)	All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences, and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
b)	Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years 2022, 2023, 2024.
Response to Request “a”:
Enclosed please find a partial production of records relating to this request.  Due to the volume of this request and the need to review such records to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists, please be advised the District requires additional time to process and complete its response to this request, as set forth below.     
With respect to the partial production, your request is granted in part and denied in part, insofar as the requested records are being provided to you with redactions, as explained below:
•	Public Officers Law §87(2)(i) permits an agency to deny access to records or portions thereof that, “if disclosed, would jeopardize the capacity of an agency or an entity that has shared information with an agency to guarantee the security of its information technology assets, such assets encompassing both electronic information systems and infrastructures.” Disclosure of information such as bank account numbers and check numbers could enable a person to gain access to or alter or adversely affect electronic information or electronic information systems, as such, they may justifiably be withheld.  § 87(2)(i). See FOIL-AO-14921 (2004); FOIL-AO-13948 (2003); FOIL-AO-13143 (2002); FOIL-AO-F9325 (1996).
•	Portions of the requested records which, if disclosed, would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy have been withheld and/or identifying details redacted pursuant to Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b), 89(2).  
Accordingly, the enclosed records have been redacted in accordance with the above. 
As stated above, the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.  In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As to the remainder of your request, it is anticipated the District will either grant or deny, in whole or in part, your request on or before March 5, 2025.  

Response to Request “b”:
Enclosed please find a partial production of records relating to this request.  Due to the volume of this request and the need to review such records to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists, please be advised the District requires additional time to process and complete its response to this request, as set forth below.     
With respect to the partial production, your request is granted in part and denied in part, insofar as the requested records are being provided to you with redactions, as explained below:
•	Public Officers Law §87(2)(i) permits an agency to deny access to records or portions thereof that, “if disclosed, would jeopardize the capacity of an agency or an entity that has shared information with an agency to guarantee the security of its information technology assets, such assets encompassing both electronic information systems and infrastructures.”  
Accordingly, the enclosed records have been redacted in accordance with the above. 
As stated above, the District requires additional time to respond to and process your request.  In determining a reasonable time for granting or denying a request, agencies, such as the District, shall “[1] consider the volume of a request, [2] the ease or difficulty in locating, retrieving or generating records, [3] the complexity of the request, [4] the need to review records to determine the extent to which they must be disclosed, [5] the number of requests received by the agency, [6] and similar factors that bear on an agency’s ability to grant access to records promptly and within a reasonable time.”  The following factors are applicable here: one, four, five, and six.  Specifically, your request seeks records that must be reviewed to determine whether an exception to FOIL exists and requires substantial effort to prepare (i.e., copy, scan and redact).  This is in addition to the other tasks the District must undertake. 
As to the remainder of your request, it is anticipated the District will either grant or deny, in whole or in part, your request on or before March 5, 2025.  
Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in whole or in part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr. Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road, Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).
					
			
Sincerely, 

DIANE CASTONGUAY
Records Access Officer
Enclosures

As you know, on February 4, 2025 and March 5, 2025, the District provided

a)      All expense reports and receipts related to retreats, conferences,
and other events attended by the five trustees of East Williston School
District Board of Education for the years 2022, 2023, 2024.

b)      Any contracts, agreements, or invoices related to the organization
and execution of these retreats, conferences, and events during the years
2022, 2023, 2024.

On June 27, 2025, July 16, 2025, August 13, 2025, September 11, 2025, and
October 9, 2025, the District provided a letter making a good faith effort
to discern the parameters of your document requests and requested
clarification of your requests.  The District has not received a response

below our prior requests for clarification.

your document requests and is requesting clarification of your requests
with respect to the years for expense reports, receipts, contracts,
agreements or invoices referred to in requests “a” and “b” above.
Specifically, the District maintains its records on a school year basis
from July 1st through June 30th.  Your request indicates specific years but
does not specify whether you are referring to the calendar year or the
school year. We request clarification as to the time parameters of your
requests.



Please advise the undersigned if you intend to clarify your request.  To
provide you with additional time to clarify, we are extending the timeline
for the District’s response to December 9, 2025.







Pursuant to FOIL, if it is determined that your request is denied in whole
or in part, you have the right to appeal a denial of any portion of your
request within thirty (30) days to the Superintendent of Schools, Dr.
Danielle Gately, East Williston Union Free School District, 11 Bacon Road,
Old Westbury, New York 11568. You will be advised in writing of the
Superintendent’s decision of your appeal within ten (10) business days
following receipt of any written appeal. Pub. Off. Law § 89(4)(a).



Regards,

Diane Castonguay, C.P.A.

East Williston School District

The District is making a good faith effort to discern the parameters of
your document requests and is requesting clarification of your requests

from July 1st through June 30th.  Your request indicates specific years but

requests.

title: Request to Department of Defense for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Department of Defense for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the U.S. Department of War has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Contract and documents related to SmartPass

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Contract and documents related to SmartPass
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

"The Jayhawk Weekly" newsletter dated September 7, 2025 describes the SmartPass System that was adopted by District 97 middle schools. I am seeking materials related to the district's use of SmartPass. Specifically, I am requesting the following:

1.  All contracts, licenses, Memorandums of Understanding, data agreements, services agreement, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents for the SmartPass system that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and the day this request is fulfilled.
    
2.  All usage policies, data retention policies, instructions to staff, and other materials that describe the District’s implementation and use of SmartPass.
    
3.  User guide or similar for the SmartPass system.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RTL

title: Panattoni Email Metadata

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Panattoni Email Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for all email communications which included anyone with a Panattoni email (*@Panattoni.com) as either the sender and/or receiver, including being CCed or BCCed, between 01/01/2025 to the date this request is processed.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the FOIA Records Request. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation:

* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation. In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution, and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: West Virginia DOC Prison-Month Level

title: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RE: Wisconsin Open Records Request — Ames/Hoiland Altercation, March 18, 2026 — Candidates Debate

Dear Chief 

Pursuant to Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(1)(a) and the Wisconsin Public Records Law, I hereby request access to and copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Lake Geneva Police Department relating to an altercation that occurred on March 18, 2026, between Cheri Ames and Joel Hoiland at or in connection with the Lake Geneva aldermanic candidates debate held that evening.

RECORDS REQUESTED

I request all of the following records without limitation as to form or format:

1. Incident and Police Reports
All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, narratives, and the final investigative report and conclusions prepared by any officer involved in responding to or investigating the March 18, 2026 altercation.

2. Video and Audio Recordings
All video recordings, body-worn camera footage, dashcam recordings, surveillance video, and any other audio or visual recordings captured by Lake Geneva Police Department personnel in connection with this incident, including recordings made at or near the debate venue.

3. Witness and Party Statements
All written, recorded, or transcribed statements obtained from any party, witness, or individual in connection with the investigation of this altercation, including but not limited to statements from Cheri Ames, Joel Hoiland, and civilian witness Joe Esposito.

4. Joe Esposito Witness Statement and Related Records
All records relating to the statement provided by civilian witness Joe Esposito, including his written or recorded statement, any follow-up communications between Mr. Esposito and the Department, and any records reflecting how his account was considered in the investigation.

5. Communications
All internal communications, emails, text messages, radio logs, CAD (Computer Aided Dispatch) records, and dispatch audio relating to the incident, including the initial call for service, officer response, and any subsequent departmental communications regarding the matter.

6. Investigative Notes and Materials
All field notes, investigative memoranda, photographs, and any other documents created or collected in the course of investigating this incident.

7. Final Report and Conclusions
The final police department report, findings, and conclusions resulting from the investigation of this altercation, including any supervisory review or approval of those conclusions.

8. Disposition Records
Any records reflecting the outcome of the investigation, including any referrals to the Walworth County District Attorney's office, charging decisions, declination of prosecution, or other final disposition of the matter.

FORMAT AND DELIVERY

I request that records be provided in electronic format (PDF or native digital format) via email. If any records exist only in physical form, I request legible copies. If electronic delivery is not practicable, please advise and I will arrange for alternative delivery.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
Gary - In response to your open records request dated April 1, 2026, for “Ames/Hoiland altercation, March 18, 2026 – candidates debate,”  I will be forwarding you a link from Axon/Evidence.com, which contains the call detail reports, bodycam videos and audio recordings pertaining to cases 26-4399, 26-4661, 26-4695, 26-4697 and 26-4702. Because the election is next week, I felt forwarding you all the recent complaints concerning city council members promptly would be helpful.

If you would like a more extensive search completed for the items not included in this link, please let me know.  I am also attaching a memo that the Chief put out to our department concerning this matter.

In an effort to protect all persons involved in this case, personally identifiable information as described in Wisconsin State Statute 19.62(5), i.e.: telephone number, street address, VIN and date of birth have been redacted pursuant to § 19.35(1)(am) in all police reports for all subjects. Additionally, the squad computers were blurred as there was information returned from DOT on the screen.  The DPPA (Driver Privacy Protection Act) prohibits a DMV from disclosing records that contain personal information that identifies an individual. This includes: name, address, telephone number, driver identification number, social security number, photographs, and medical or disability information. In an effort to comply with the legal requirements set forth by the DPPA, the following information is redacted by our department prior to any record being released: street and/or mailing address, city, telephone number(s), date of birth, driver’s license number, vehicle identification number, vehicle license plate number, and any medical or disability information. As it pertains to the above listed record, all personal information was obtained from DMV records and therefore, must be redacted to comply with DPPA requirements.

I have also redacted the identity of the anonymous witness in Case 26-4661, and any information that may identify the witness for the following reason: the records custodian must consider the legislative intent to ensure that “all witnesses of crime are treated with dignity, respect, courtesy and sensitivity; and that the rights extended in [Chapter 950] to victims and witnesses of crime are honored and protected by law enforcement agencies, prosecutors and judges.” §950.01, Wis. Stats. Preventing witnesses and victims from being revictimized by disclosure outweighs the public’s right to know their identities.

Additionally, please be advised that the definition of record under the Public Records Law does not include a draft, note, or preliminary form of a document produced by a municipal employee or officer for that individual’s personal use. Wis. Stat. § 19.32(2). Thus, notes kept in a police officer’s memo book are not subject to disclosure pursuant to the Public Records Law if they are considered to be for his or her personal use or to aid his or her recollection. The officers’ notebooks were redacted from body-worn camera video for this reason. I have included the Call Details Report Chronological Order, which details summaries from officers and dispatchers of activities during a police contact. Sometimes these are called “notes”, however, because they are created as a narrative and not solely for the officers’ personal recollection, therefore, those were released.

Additionally, to protect the personal privacy and safety of department members, body-worn camera video was redacted when featuring cell phone, the inside of the police department and the exterior of anonymous witness’ residence.

As I am sure you are aware, if you are not satisfied with this response, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(a), Wisconsin Statutes, bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record.  Additionally, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(b)m, request the Walworth County District Attorney or the Attorney General to bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record. Should you need anything else, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Lori Reynolds
Police Records Specialist
Lake Geneva Police Dept.
626 Geneva St.
Lake Geneva, WI 53147
(262) 248-4455

title: ALPR audit

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci Phelps

communications.subject: Re: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
Hello,
This correspondence is in response to your Sunshine Law request, reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS.
After researching the request, the estimated amount of time required to fulfill this request is approximately 4 hours. Pursuant to Section 610.026, RSMo, the cost for staff time is $21.00 per hour, for a total estimated cost of $84.00.
Section 610.026, RSMo, authorizes public governmental bodies to charge for the actual cost of document research, duplication, and staff time required to fulfill a Sunshine Law request.
Please be advised that any records released will be reviewed and may require redaction of information exempt from disclosure under Missouri law, including but not limited to information related to active investigations, confidential records, or other sensitive information as permitted by law.
If you wish to proceed with this request, please remit payment in the amount of $84.00 to our office and reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS. Upon receipt of payment, processing of your request will begin.
If you choose not to proceed, no further action will be taken. Should you have any questions regarding this estimate, please feel free to contact our office.
Thank you,

[cid:472ac147-2c3b-4017-815e-6e57993528b0]

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office Return to Office Communications 2025
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith -

In response to your request below, except as set forth herein, attached please find records responsive to your request.

Certain records have been withheld to the extent they are exempt from disclosure under 1 V.S.A. 317(c)(4) as executive privileged. This includes those records advisory to or from the Governor and the Governor's Senior Staff with respect to policy decisions involving the State's Hybrid Work Standard prepared for the purpose of formulating policy and making decisions regarding matters for which the Agency of Administration, members of the Governor's Cabinet, the Tax Department and the Department of Labor were responsible.

You may also be aware that the State has been sued by VSEA over this issue. We are consulting with the Attorney General's Office regarding releasing communications from State employees.

Jaye Pershing Johnson, the Governor's Legal Counsel, is responsible for denial of the privileged and confidential records. I conferred with Ms. Johnson when making the determination to redact or withhold information.

As the records custodian, I certify, as required by 1 V.S.A. 318(a)(2), that I consider the redacted and withheld materials to be exempt from disclosure. You have the right to appeal, as explained in 1 V.S.A. 318, to Jason Gibbs, Chief of Staff, relative to my certification of the records as exempt.

Sincerely,
Karen Pallas
Records Officer

I was unaware at the time of my request legal action was pending, thank you for bringing this to my attention and the explanation as it relates to downstream response actions based on that.

Thank you,
Brandon

You do not have to make an additional request regarding the Executive Office’s records retention schedule. I will confirm with our legal counsel and send to you asap.
Best,
Karen Pallas

title: Performance Evaluation (Franklin School District/SAU18)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Franklin School District/SAU18)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation for the current district superintendent at the time of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

Good morning,

I am in receipt of your 91-A request and will need 10 business days to respond.

Sincerely,

Dan LeGallo
SAU 18 Superintendent

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Oyster River Cooperative School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Statutory Notice and Implementation Records (ORS 659A.279)

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Statutory Notice and Implementation Records (ORS 659A.279)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting a public records request for records sufficient to demonstrate how the University of Oregon satisfies the statutory notice requirement under ORS 659A.279 for the safety-related leave protections set out in ORS 659A.270–659A.285.

To be clear, this request concerns the employer-administered statutory protection itself, which exists independently of the Paid Leave Oregon wage-replacement program and applies regardless of whether leave is paid or unpaid, including public-employer obligations such as the 160-hour leave bank.

Please provide records showing the following:

1. Notice content and location
•	The specific notice(s), webpage(s), posting(s), or written materials the University relies on to “summarize the provisions” of ORS 659A.270–659A.285, as required by ORS 659A.279;
•	Where those notices are posted or maintained, including physical locations and URLs;
•	The date each notice or posting went into effect and any revisions made since 2019.

2. Procedures available to employees
•	Any written procedures, guidance, or internal instructions explaining how an employee invokes the statutory protection with the University;
•	Identification of the office(s) responsible for administering the protection;
•	Policies or guidance explaining how the protection functions when Paid Leave Oregon benefits are unavailable, exhausted, or not applicable;
•	Any materials intended to allow employees to plan around the protection, including timing, documentation, duration, and job-protection effects.

3. Transition and change records (2019–2026)
•	Records documenting the transition from earlier certification forms and processes in use in or around 2019 to later versions used in 2026;
•	Any drafts, approvals, internal communications, or decision-making records associated with revising or replacing those forms or procedures;
•	Records reflecting how the University evaluated whether revised forms, postings, or procedures continued to comply with ORS 659A.279 following the implementation of Paid Leave Oregon;
•	Any records addressing how employees were notified of changes in forms, routing, or administration.
•	Any written policies, procedures, workflows, routing instructions, or standard operating procedures used by Human Resources, HR Leaves, or other offices when an employee requests safety-related leave and Paid Leave Oregon benefits are unavailable or exhausted, including records addressing whether and how the employee is routed to University-administered unpaid leave or the 160-hour public-employer leave bank.

If the University contends that no responsive records exist for any portion of this request, please confirm that position in writing.

Clarification regarding parallel processes:

To avoid any confusion, this public records request is submitted pursuant to Oregon Public Records Law only and does not waive, replace, or otherwise affect any requests, grievances, or RFIs I have submitted through my union or any other employment-related process, many of which remain pending.

This request is submitted because I require timely access to records necessary to understand my statutory protections and to plan accordingly. The public records process is the only mechanism currently available to obtain this information within a reasonable timeframe.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Kelvin Shivers II

Dear Mr. Shivers: 
The University of Oregon, Office of Public Records has received your public records request for "I am submitting a public records request for records sufficient to demonstrate how the University of Oregon satisfies the statutory notice requirement under ORS 659A.279 for the safety-related leave protections set out in ORS 659A.270–659A.285.To be clear, this request concerns the employer-administered statutory protection itself, which exists independently of the Paid Leave Oregon wage-replacement program and applies regardless of whether leave is paid or unpaid, including public-employer obligations such as the 160-hour leave bank.Please provide records showing the following:1. Notice content and location• The specific notice(s), webpage(s), posting(s), or written materials the University relies on to “summarize the provisions” of ORS 659A.270–659A.285, as required by ORS 659A.279;• Where those notices are posted or maintained, including physical locations and URLs;• The date each notice or posting went into effect and any revisions made since 2019.2. Procedures available to employees• Any written procedures, guidance, or internal instructions explaining how an employee invokes the statutory protection with the University;• Identification of the office(s) responsible for administering the protection;• Policies or guidance explaining how the protection functions when Paid Leave Oregon benefits are unavailable, exhausted, or not applicable;• Any materials intended to allow employees to plan around the protection, including timing, documentation, duration, and job-protection effects.3. Transition and change records (2019–2026)• Records documenting the transition from earlier certification forms and processes in use in or around 2019 to later versions used in 2026;• Any drafts, approvals, internal communications, or decision-making records associated with revising or replacing those forms or procedures;• Records reflecting how the University evaluated whether revised forms, postings, or procedures continued to comply with ORS 659A.279 following the implementation of Paid Leave Oregon;• Any records addressing how employees were notified of changes in forms, routing, or administration.• Any written policies, procedures, workflows, routing instructions, or standard operating procedures used by Human Resources, HR Leaves, or other offices when an employee requests safety-related leave and Paid Leave Oregon benefits are unavailable or exhausted, including records addressing whether and how the employee is routed to University-administered unpaid leave or the 160-hour public-employer leave bank." on 02/09/2026, attached. If documents exist and can be provided, the office will send the documents electronically to avoid a copy fee of 25 cents per page. The office also charges for the actual cost of making public records available. The charge includes, but is not limited to, staff costs for locating, gathering, summarizing, compiling, reviewing, tailoring or redacting the public records to respond to a request. The charge may also include the cost of time spent by an attorney in reviewing the public records, redacting material from the public records, or segregating the public records into exempt and nonexempt records. 
The cost of time for each employee is calculated by multiplying the employee's hourly wage calculation (including benefits expenses) by the hours or portions thereof necessary to locate, gather, summarize, compile, tailor, review, redact, segregate, certify or attend the inspection of the public records requested. 
Thank you for contacting the University with your request. Sincerely, 
Office of Public Records 6207 University of Oregon |Eugene, OR 97403-6207 (541) 346-6823 | pubrec@uoregon.edu publicrecords.uoregon.edu

communications.communication:
UO Public Records Office,

Thank you for the estimate.

To further narrow the scope, I am requesting only information sufficient to identify where the University has provided the statutory notice required under ORS 659A.279 and ORS 659A.283.

Specifically, please provide:
• The physical location(s) where this notice has been posted or made available to employees;
• The digital location(s) (webpages, intranet pages, policy libraries, or similar) where the notice has been published; and
• The date(s) the notice was first posted or published, and, if applicable, the date(s) it was removed, revised, or replaced.

I am not requesting drafts, internal correspondence, or legal review materials—only records identifying the locations and posting history of the operative notice.

Because this request seeks to understand how a public university has implemented a statutory employee-notice obligation, I request consideration of a fee waiver or reduction in the public interest. The information is being sought to evaluate public-facing compliance, not for a commercial purpose.

For record-keeping purposes, please direct all further correspondence regarding this request through the MuckRock portal rather than my work email.

Please let me know if this narrowed scope results in a revised estimate.

Best, 
Kelvin Shivers II

communications.subject: University of Oregon Office of Public Records 2026-PRR-394
communications.communication:
02/20/2026 Dear Mr. Shivers: The University of Oregon has received your amended public records request for • The physical location(s) where this notice has been posted or made available to employees; • The digital location(s) (webpages, intranet pages, policy libraries, or similar) where the notice has been published; and • The date(s) the notice was first posted or published, and, if applicable, the date(s) it was removed, revised, or replaced. " on 02/10/2026, attached. With this email, the office is providing you with an estimate to respond to your requests. 

The office estimates the actual cost of responding to your request to be $266.03. Upon receipt of a check made payable to the University of Oregon for that amount, the office will proceed to locate, copy, and provide the records you have requested that are not exempt from disclosure. Your check may be sent to the attention of Office of Public Records, 6207 University of Oregon, Eugene, OR 97403-6207. Please note that if the cost of preparing the documents for you is less than the estimate, we will refund the difference. If the cost of preparing the records for you exceeds the estimate, however, you may be charged for the difference. Following is an outline of how costs are determined. The office will provide the documents electronically to avoid a copy fee of 25 cents per page.  The office also charges for the actual cost of making public records available.  The charge includes, but is not limited to, staff costs for locating, gathering, summarizing, compiling, reviewing, tailoring or redacting the public records to respond to a request.  The charge may also include the cost of time spent by an attorney in reviewing the public records, redacting material from the public records, or segregating the public records into exempt and nonexempt records. ***** If the request is to inspect records, the charge includes the time for records to be prepared for review and the cost of staff time for a staff member to be present while the requester reviews the records to preserve the integrity of the university’s records.  If the request is to certify the records as true copies, the charge will include employee time spent in doing so.  The charge will also include the cost of sending the records where copies are requested. ***** The cost of time for each employee is calculated by multiplying the employee's hourly wage calculation (including benefits expenses) by the hours or portions thereof necessary to locate, gather, summarize, compile, tailor, review, redact, segregate, certify or attend the inspection of the public records requested. Thank you for contacting us with your request. Sincerely, 
Office of Public Records 6207 University of Oregon | Eugene, OR 97403-6207 (541) 346-6823 | pubrec@uoregon.edu publicrecords.uoregon.edu

communications.subject: RE: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Statutory Notice and Implementation Records (ORS 659A.279)
communications.communication:
Dear Office of Public Records,

To reduce burden and cost, I am narrowing my February 10, 2026 request to two specific categories of existing records, identified below.

These records are being requested in order to inform public comment to the University of Oregon Board of Trustees and to allow the public to understand how the University has implemented and communicated statutory notice obligations affecting employees.

I intend to reference this information during the Board of Trustees meeting currently scheduled for March 17, 2026. If the records cannot be produced in advance of that date, I request that they be produced as soon as practicable thereafter so they may be included in materials available to the Board and public for subsequent review or discussion at a future meeting.

Because this request is directed at understanding institutional compliance and facilitating informed public participation in university governance, I respectfully request a fee waiver or substantial reduction pursuant to ORS 192.324(5), as the disclosure primarily benefits the general public rather than any individual requester.

To reduce burden and cost, I am narrowing the request to the following existing records:

1. Any record that contains the notice provided to employees pursuant to ORS 659A.279, including materials that communicate to employees:
   • the availability of Safe Leave under ORS 659A.270–659A.285;
   • the purposes for which Safe Leave may be used;
   • how employees request or access such leave;
   • protections against retaliation or discrimination for invoking those rights; and
   • any information the University provides to employees describing rights, responsibilities, or procedures associated with these protections.

2. Any written notice, template, standard communication, or guidance provided to employees when the University becomes aware of circumstances that may implicate protections addressed within ORS Chapter 659A, including ORS 659A.283 and related anti-retaliation provisions.

The information currently published at https://hr.uoregon.edu/safe-leave appears to provide partial guidance. This request seeks the complete notice materials and communications used in practice to inform employees of these statutory rights.

I am requesting only records that already exist and are maintained in the ordinary course of business. I am not requesting the creation of new documents, legal analysis, or certification of compliance.

Please let me know whether this narrowing affects the estimated cost and whether the fee may be waived or reduced in light of the public-interest basis described above.

Sincerely,
Kelvin Shivers II

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFQ# 398393 , 2 
Title/Description: Metro Nashville Public Schools Camera System for Bus Fleet

Issuing Entity: Metro Nashville Public Schools, Purchasing Department

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment. 

 Regards,

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Transfer Data, SY25
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Anonymized data of all school transfers of CPS students from the start of the 2025/2026 school year to December 31, 2025. These records should include a unique non-identifiable code for the student, the original school, the receiving school, and the month and year of transfer.

Include the transfer code in the above-requested data table:
1. 33 - Transfer Outside of Chicago
2. 86 - Unable to Locate
3. 31 - Transfer within CPS
4. 32- Transfer to non-public
5. 52 - Program Ended, PreK or K
6. 40 - Homeschooled
7. 35 - Legally Committed to an Institution
8. 87 - 17 or >, Cons to Wthd

If the student transferred via Code 33, include the receiving school. Note if it's a verified transfer or not.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.  The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

Email or letters containing warning letters, recall requests, 483s, or untitled letters between FDA's CDER or ORA and the following companies referencing yellow oleander contamination in supplements between January 1, 2023 to December 31, 2025

OBC Group, SdB Semilla de Brasil Tejocote Root, SdB ELITE, natural vida, SiluetaYa LLC, New Me, Shopnewmeroot.com, vidaslim co., vidaslim USA, Creativa Interiors, LLC, Privit Wellness, LLC, Innovacion Natural, LLC, World Green Nutrition Inc., Alipotec, Alipotec South TX, NWL Nutra, Global Mix Inc., Eva Nutrition, Sunset Sales +, ELV King, ALIPOTEC RAIZ DE TEJOCOTE, Natural Supplements, LLC, Science of Alpha, H and Natural, H & Natural, Backstage CTC Seller, Nutraholics ELV, Niwali, or Pastor-Villareal, Inc.

I am an investigative journalist writing for MedShadow.org.

communications.subject: Request FDA-FOIA-2026-865-HFP-1
communications.communication:
FDA-FOIA-2026-865-HFP-1 Dear: Emma Yasinski The attached record(s) are being provided by the U.S. Food & Drug Administration’s (FDA), Human Foods Program (HFP) in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request, referenced above, for record(s) from the Food and Drug Administration pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act regarding: Email or letters containing warning letters, recall requests, 483s, or untitled letters between FDA's CDER or ORA and the following companies referencing yellow oleander contamination in supplements between January 1, 2023, to December 31, 2025. OBC Group, SdB Semilla de Brasil Tejocote Root, SdB ELITE, natural vida, SiluetaYa LLC, New Me, Shopnewmeroot.com, vidaslim co., vidaslim USA, Creativa Interiors, LLC, Privit Wellness, LLC, Innovacion Natural, LLC, World Green Nutrition Inc., Alipotec, Alipotec South TX, NWL Nutra, Global Mix Inc., Eva Nutrition, Sunset Sales +, ELV King, ALIPOTEC RAIZ DE TEJOCOTE, Natural Supplements, LLC, Science of Alpha, H and Natural, H & Natural, Backstage CTC Seller, Nutraholics ELV, Niwali, or Pastor-Villareal, Inc.. Your request is granted in full. We are enclosing 16 pages with this response. This is HFP’s final response to your FOIA request. If you have any questions about this response, you may contact me at kimberly.jones@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:kimberly.jones@fda.hhs.gov) or by phone 240-402-1825. 
Please note, your request is assigned to additional office(s) within FDA. A separate response will be sent by the additional FDA office(s). In accordance with 45 CFR § 5.61 and 21 CFR § 20.41(b)(5), you have the right to appeal this determination. Your appeal should clearly identify the agency determination that is being appealed. It would be helpful if you provide specific reasons explaining why you believe the agency's adverse determination should be reconsidered. By filing an appeal, you preserve your rights under FOIA and give the agency a chance to review and reconsider your request and the agency’s decision. Your appeal must be mailed or emailed within 90 days from the date of this response, to: Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services U.S. Food & Drug Administration 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050 Rockville, MD 20857 FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) . Please clearly mark both the envelope and your letter “FDA Freedom of Information Act Appeal.” Items arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time will be deemed received on the next workday. If you would like to discuss our response before filing an appeal to attempt to resolve your dispute without going through the appeals process, please contact Ms. Charis Wilson at 240-402-9116. You may also contact the FDA FOIA Public Liaison for assistance at: Director, Office of Management and Enterprise Services, US Food & Drug Administration, 5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1050, Rockville, MD 20857, email: FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov (mailto:FDAFOIA@fda.hhs.gov) . If you are unable to resolve your FOIA dispute through our FOIA Public Liaison, the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS), the Federal FOIA Ombudsman’s office, offers mediation services to help resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road–OGIS, College Park, MD 20740-6001; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769; email at ogis@nara.gov (mailto:ogis@nara.gov) . The following charges for this request to date may be included in a monthly invoice. Please do not submit payment unless you receive an invoice

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CPS Directory, March 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

A CPS directory listing school/unit, title, phone number and e-mail address for each CPS official, staff or employee with an email address. This request includes board members, subcommittee members, part-time employees, parent workers and any person issued a CPS email address.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. title: NOVA CC - FOIA Request

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (Va. Code §2.2-3700 et seq.), I am requesting a dataset containing course grade distributions for Northern Virginia Community College.

Specifically, I request records for each course section (from the last 5 years) by academic term including:
Semester and Year of Course
Subject code and course number
Course title
Section number
Instructor name
Number of students receiving each grade (A, B, C, D, F, W, etc.)
Total enrollment
Average GPA for the section (if available)

If possible, I request that the data be provided in a machine-readable format such as CSV or Excel.
If your office is not the custodian of these records, please forward this request to the appropriate office.

Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with John Dowell.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Nash:

I am the FOIA officer for Northern Virginia Community College, and I am in receipt of your request submitted under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act seeking grade distribution for each course at NOVA.

NOVA charges a fee of $25 per hour for FOIA services. (See our FOIA page for details<https://www.nvcc.edu/about/offices/system-counsel/foia.html>.) This request is likely to take a significant amount of time to fulfill, so I am invoking the need for additional time as allowed by the statute ( §2.2-3704.B.4<https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title2.2/chapter37/section2.2-3704/>). Once I hear back from you that you are willing to pay associated FOIA fees, I will move forward with your request.

Don't hesitate to reach out to FOIA@nvcc.edu<mailto:FOIA@nvcc.edu> should you have questions.

Sincerely,

KATHY A. THOMPSON
Assoc. Director of Business Communications
   & College FOIA Officer
Business Engagement
Office of Strategy, Research and Workforce Innovation
4001 Wakefield Chapel Road, Rm. 368, Annandale, VA 22003
Office: 703.323.2168 | Cell: 703.855.5556
KAThompson@nvcc.edu<mailto:KAThompson@nvcc.edu>  |  nvcc.edu<http://www.nvcc.edu/>

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NOVA CC - FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Thompson,

Thank you for your response.

To help minimize the time and cost required to fulfill this request, I would like to narrow the scope. If possible, please provide data for the past three academic years instead of five. Please let me know whether this change would reduce the cost of fulfilling the request.

Additionally, if the requested information already exists in a precompiled format (such as internal reports, dashboards, or database exports), I would be happy to receive those existing records rather than requesting that a new dataset be created.

Before proceeding, could you please provide an estimate of the expected cost to fulfill the request, as well as the approximate number of hours required?

Thank you for your assistance.
