title: Lake Washington School District - Changes and Revisions of Board Policy 4040 and 4040P - Public Access to District Records (2025)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Lake Washington School District - Changes and Revisions of Board Policy 4040 and 4040P - Public Access to District Records (2025)
communications.communication:
Lake Washington School District - Changes and Revisions of Board Policy 4040 and 4040P - Public Access to District Records (2025)

October 06, 2025

To: PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST – RCW 42.56

To whom it may concern,

Pursuant to the Washington State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56), I hereby request the following identifiable public records:

1. Copies of any and all records and writings relating to any change, proposed change, revision, amendment, discussion, review, adoption, or repeal of LWSD Board Policy 4040 (“Public Access to District Records”) and LWSD Procedure 4040P (“Public Access to District Records – Procedure”) during the calendar year 2025.

This request includes, but is not limited to, the following categories of records:

Drafts, redlines, markups, or tracked-change versions of Policy 4040 or Procedure 4040P circulated internally or externally.

Emails, memoranda, notes, or correspondence (including electronic messages or calendar items) discussing proposed or actual edits, updates, or reviews of Policy 4040/4040P.

Meeting agendas, minutes, recordings, notes, or presentations of the LWSD Board of Directors, policy committees, or district staff meetings where Policy 4040 or 4040P was discussed.

Recommendations or input from legal counsel, consultants, WSSDA, or other agencies regarding Policy 4040/4040P.

Final or adopted versions of Policy 4040 and 4040P, as amended or reaffirmed in 2025.

Public communications or notices announcing or explaining any updates or actions taken in 2025 related to these policies.

Format of Production & Delivery

- Provide all records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF, XLSX, DOCX).
- If necessary, bundle responsive records into the fewest files possible using free .zip compression.
- Production should be in reverse chronological order (most recent record produced first).
- Delivery Method:
  - Direct email attachment, or
  - Direct internet download link with no password and no registration required.
- Expressly prohibited: Do not require use of JustFOIA, GovQA, NextRequest, FOIAOnline, or any similar third-party portal.

Exemption Logs

If any redactions or withholdings are made, the District must provide a detailed exemption log, identifying:
- The statutory RCW exemption invoked.
- The record type and specific withheld portion.
- The number of invoices, pages, or documents withheld in whole or redacted.

Fees

If fees apply to the production of this request, please provide an itemized fee estimate in advance. Artificial inflation of costs by fragmenting or unbundling records that could reasonably be produced together is not permissible under RCW 42.56.120.

Preservation of Records

This request anticipates litigation. Under RCW 42.56.100 and WAC 44-14-03005, the District must preserve all potentially responsive invoices and related records until this request is fully resolved. Destruction of responsive records after receipt of this request may constitute a violation of RCW 40.16.010 and RCW 40.16.020.

Exemption Challenge 

- Public Record Status: All fee invoices and billing records are “writings” under RCW 42.56.010(4), as they are prepared, used, or retained by the District in connection with the performance of a governmental function.

- No Blanket Exemption: There is no statutory exemption for public records fee invoices. Exemptions must be specific, narrowly applied, and accompanied by a detailed exemption log (Sanders v. State, 169 Wn.2d 827 (2010)).

- Judicial Review: If LWSD denies access, I will seek judicial review under RCW 42.56.550, including recovery of attorney fees and statutory penalties per day per record unlawfully withheld (Yousoufian v. Office of Ron Sims, 168 Wn.2d 444 (2010)).

Commercial Purpose Declaration

This request is not made for any commercial purpose and does not seek a “list of individuals” as prohibited by RCW 42.56.070(8).

Please provide responsive records within five business days as required by RCW 42.56.

Good Luck.

Preservation of Records

This request anticipates litigation. Under RCW 42.56.100 and WAC 44-14-03005, the District must preserve all potentially responsive invoices and related records until this request is fully resolved. Destruction of responsive records after receipt of this request may constitute a violation of RCW 40.16.010 and RCW 40.16.020.

Exemption Challenge

- Public Record Status: All fee invoices and billing records are “writings” under RCW 42.56.010(4), as they are prepared, used, or retained by the District in connection with the performance of a governmental function.

- Judicial Review: If LWSD denies access, I will seek judicial review under RCW 42.56.550, including recovery of attorney fees and statutory penalties per day per record unlawfully withheld (Yousoufian v. Office of Ron Sims, 168 Wn.2d 444 (2010)).

Commercial Purpose Declaration

This request is not made for any commercial purpose and does not seek a “list of individuals” as prohibited by RCW 42.56.070(8).

Please provide responsive records within five business days as required by RCW 42.56.

Good Luck.

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2102168/

Filed via MuckRock.com

E-mail (Preferred): 194242-75454030@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:194242-75454030@requests.muckrock.com>

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS
For mailed responses, please address (see note):
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 194242
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.subject: Lake Washington School DIstrict, WA. Fee issued notification for PRR-2025-249
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 This email concerns the public records request, Request Number: PRR-2025-249 , you submitted that was received by Lake Washington School District on Monday, October 6, 2025. A fee has been issued for your request for records. 
  
 Following the submission of your request, a preliminary search was completed. The estimated volume of records and associated costs are detailed in the invoice provided. District policy requires that you be provided with a summary of applicable anticipated charges before charges are imposed. This is to allow the requestor to revise the request in an effort to reduce the applicable charges. To this end, a detailed invoice is provided separately for your review. Please click here to view: Request Number: PRR-2025-249 
  
 Please be advised that District policy/procedure [https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdDT6VWnYPCfrNw8GE28dNLFru-2BD5tK9Zn73Cfsmpb5xfQNNDYtr1dMm5vktmeCj0iSrGxGsTFS4GDzVEhwn74I-3D5Z6x_WlqKI5s4KtFad2-2F2O-2FVuone3vcLeXmwHLhY7chD5ZoAilUf4xnTzW7w2sgMCDrrb173i3SPtVlan-2Bi0FTsBud5QBYCJLcFSzr5PYDW4QwRzx8qK6scnMR7U0kFo6BVPC4pHbY1EahjGwYgvVdTEnQP9zv9uMA2s7ZwOO1kSfTiyey3znCM-2F1TjkOcKtoHmE0pW4UoHb-2BDephVtv1E8kJcxruaAtZJUigYpw0GCkjA6bmsn7FplhWMvt9BXMuMdkHSUyxT-2BWdO1HMrXycdsW366k-2FsU1NnHGSt2KsPcQoCmKEB4FO20h9VFXQAWldHYF-2B ] contains the following schedule of charges that may apply to your request: 
  
 1. Ten cents per page for public records scanned into an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to scan the records;
2. Five cents per each four electronic files or attachments uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery;
3. Ten cents per gigabyte for the transmission of public records in an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to send the records electronically; and
4. The actual cost of any digital storage media or device provided by the District, the actual cost of any container or envelope used to mail the copies to the requestor, and the actual postage or delivery charge. 
  
 Alternatively, if the District determines and documents that the fees allowed under this procedure are clearly equal to, or less, than two dollars, the District may instead charge a flat fee of two dollars to provide the records. If the District charges a flat fee for the first installment, the District will not charge an additional flat fee or a per page fee for any subsequent installments. 
  
 Due to the volume of records, the District is exercising its discretion under District procedure and RCW 42.56.120(4) to require a deposit of ten percent of the estimated charges associated with this request.  
  
 After payment is received, we will be authorized by District procedure and the Public Records Act to begin reviewing the responsive records. At that point, we will provide you with the anticipated date of an initial installment. 
  
 Please be advised that we are required to allow you a 30-day time frame to make the pre-payment as outlined above, measured from the date of this email. A failure to receive such payment from a requester within that time frame permits us to consider their request abandoned and close it. Also, the District would also be amenable to narrowing the scope of your request in an effort to reduce the associated fees the District intends to assess. 
  
 Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns about the foregoing or any aspect of the District's work to fulfill your request. 
  
 To view your request status, please click Request Number: PRR-2025-249 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 Public Records Office 
 15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, WA 98052 
 publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org

title: Sausalito 2025 RIPA

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sausalito 2025 RIPA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all records reflecting data collected and recorded by the Police Department in accordance with the California Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) from January 1, 2025, through the present. Please provide the raw data in excel or csv format.

2) Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of, the City/Town that pertain to any RIPA data that has been collected by the Police Department since January 1, 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

communications.subject: Your City of Sausalito public records request #26-25 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
City of Sausalito ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-25 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for your request. Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, Government Code Section 7920.000, et seq., (the “Act”), this is to confirm receipt of your request for records you sent to the City of Sausalito (“The City”).   Under Government Code 7922.535, the City has 10-days from the receipt of the request, to make a determination as to whether it possesses responsive records, unless there is a basis for an extension. You may email CityClerk@sausalito.gov if you have not received any response from the City after 10 days of submitting your request. Please note that the Public Records Act, the City is under no obligation to create or produce records that do not already exist at the time of request. The City will not produce records that are not subject to disclosure under the Public Records Act, and may redact records to protect non-disclosable information. If the City determines it is in possession of disclosable public records, the records will be provided electronically free of charge. Request for paper copies of releasable public record must be accompanied by fees to cover copying cost at $0.25 per page. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Sausalito.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

title: Command Central CPD

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Command Central CPD
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Records sufficient to show any emails sent or received by any CPD email address, between Jan 2, 2023 and the present date, that includes either "commandcentral" or "command central" that ALSO has "flock" or "flocksafety". 

For the purposes of this request "includes" means "is contained -- partially or otherwise -- within either the subject line, the email body, or any of the email addresses".

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

LPL

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (University Of Tennessee At Chattanooga)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (University Of Tennessee At Chattanooga)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: AER - CO Dept. of Public Health and Env.

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: AER - CO Dept. of Public Health and Env.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse event records filed with the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan

title: NCAA Financial Report (College of William and Mary)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NCAA Financial Report (College of William and Mary)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009 & 2024.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Pennsylvania HHS response

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Pennsylvania HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Pennsylvania Department of Health remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Pennsylvania Department of Health employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: DOH - RTKL
communications.communication:
The Pennsylvania Department of Health's Right-to-Know Law (RTKL) Office has received your correspondence. Requests for records will be processed in accordance with the provisions of the RTKL, 65 P.S.§§67.101, et seq.

The Department is deemed to have received the request on the business day that the AORO actually receives the request. See 65 P.S. § 67.901. This includes after regular business hours (5 pm) or when the Office is otherwise closed pursuant to Management Directive 530.17 and will be marked as received on the next business day.

title: Auror (Tukwila Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Auror (Tukwila Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All emails and email notifications sent or received by agency personnel that include the domain "@auror.co". This includes but is not limited to notifications of incident reports, evidence exports, event updates, and other communications generated by the Auror software.
2. All exported reports generated by the Auror software. This includes reports exported in any format (PDF, HTML, JSON, etc.) and covers all types of reports such as evidence reports, incident reports.
3. All incident of investigative reports mentioning or involving the Auror software. This includes any reports or records where the Auror software was used to document, track, or investigate incidents.
4.  SOW, contracts, MOU, user manuals or technical documentation regarding the Auror platform. 

To assist with locating these records, the Auror software is a platform used for tracking, monitoring, and managing various incidents and retail crime investigations. It includes functionalities for exporting reports and sending email notifications.

Responsive documents should be from 1/1/2020 until the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

2. All exported reports generated by the Auror software. This includes reports exported in any format (PDF, HTML, JSON, etc.) and covers all types of reports such as evidence reports, incident reports. 
3. All incident of investigative reports mentioning or involving the Auror software. This includes any reports or records where the Auror software was used to document, track, or investigate incidents. 
4. SOW, contracts, MOU, user manuals or technical documentation regarding the Auror platform. 

To assist with locating these records, the Auror software is a platform used for tracking, monitoring, and managing various incidents and retail crime investigations. It includes functionalities for exporting reports and sending email notifications.

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of December 06, 2025., Reference # P034703-120625. 
Dear Rose  Terse, 
The Tukwila Police Department received a public records request from you on December 06, 2025 via our electronic Records Center. Your request mentioned: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All emails and email notifications sent or received by agency personnel that include the domain "@auror.co". This includes but is not limited to notifications of incident reports, evidence exports, event updates, and other communications generated by the Auror software. 
2. All exported reports generated by the Auror software. This includes reports exported in any format (PDF, HTML, JSON, etc.) and covers all types of reports such as evidence reports, incident reports. 
3. All incident of investigative reports mentioning or involving the Auror software. This includes any reports or records where the Auror software was used to document, track, or investigate incidents. 
4. SOW, contracts, MOU, user manuals or technical documentation regarding the Auror platform. 

To assist with locating these records, the Auror software is a platform used for tracking, monitoring, and managing various incidents and retail crime investigations. It includes functionalities for exporting reports and sending email notifications. 

Responsive documents should be from 1/1/2020 until the date this request is processed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2146581/" 
The department has reviewed your request and has determined that some or all of the records requested are exempt from disclosure under the Public Records Act for the following reasons: 
06a Investigative, Law enforcement, crime victims RCW 42.56.240(1) Specific intelligence information and investigative records are exemption from disclosure. 
07b Identifying Information of Juvenile Offender RCW 13.50.050(3) and (5); RCW 42.56.070(1) Identifying information of a juvenile contained in a record related to the commission of a juvenile offense is confidential and may not be released. 
Please note there are black boxes (redactions) on various pages of the responsive documents. The black box may be small or large, depending on the size of the redaction. There may be multiple redactions on a page. Within the redaction is a white code, which will look something along the lines of “Redacted Text [1a].” This code designates the statutory exemption being applied to the document. If there is only one code on the page, but there are numerous redactions, that one statutory exemption applies to all the redactions. 
Provided with these responsive documents is a key to the codes. This key identifies the statutory bases for the exemption as well as a brief explanation for why the exemption applies. As you review these responsive documents, you will be able to refer to the key below to identify the exemption and its application. 
You should now be able to retrieve any responsive records via the Tukwila PD electronic Record Center. 
The records are best viewed on a desktop or laptop computer. The records may not be accessible from a mobile device. The Police Department has provided you with a web link to all records that are responsive to your request.  The Police Department now considers your request as closed, and, therefore does not intend to further address the request.  Any actions to seek judicial review of the City’s response to your request must be filed within one year of this notice in accordance with RCW 42.56.550. 
If you believe that additional responsive records have not been provided or if have any questions, you may contact our office at (206) 433-1808 or email the Police Department through your GovQA account within 30 days of this message. 
Regards, 
Rebecca 
Tukwila Police Department 
206-433-1808

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

All incident reports between the years 2022-2025 of the address 8521 S. Jeffery Blvd, Chicago, IL, 60617

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Young

communications.subject: Welcome to the City of Chicago Request Center!
communications.communication:
Dear Brandon  Young, 
 
 
Thank you for registering with the City of Chicago Public Records Portal. 
If you did not submit your request through the public portal but are receiving this e-mail, we are excited to share with you that you can now submit FOIA requests through a newly implemented online request system. While you will still be allowed to email your FOIA requests, creating an account and submitting your request online will allow you to check the online portal for the status of requests. You will also receive an email when the records are ready to be downloaded. 
 
Please click here to visit our (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXT2aAhmhBYiUcSShAuSz-2Fsuc8QsVv36mciHGQCY-2FePau6i2w_5PSW-2FzNMZEaKKBJqXPbzwaDYkgTA1uhQM2HlgJwBdfaLvk-2Bewt-2FnFFkFOe2OPbZbUSLAu73q6uYrDgrnFcQQGeNEQFoSV5F9filWBiioL4oWaLsIEoHIJkdXRZLrhBp1sb4GvNzbGwe6CFbtJk-2F49jG-2BzPctb0Dnqjo5tVlRn-2FmA37nESSpgiXbDZvTd2z2zpwJ5PQ5IecnPH07dzyAauD2vo5WApi2vN5NRHpVU4n4-3D) Chicago Public Records portal! communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: P127739-121525
communications.communication:
Dear Brandon : 
 
Thank you for your interest in information from the Chicago Police Department. Your FOIA request has been received and is being processed. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: P127739-121525 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

All incident reports between the years 2022-2025 of the address 8521 S. Jeffery Blvd, Chicago, IL, 60617 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Brandon Young 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2154698/ 
Chicago Police Department (CPD) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq.  If further time is needed to assemble and copy all documents responsive to your FOIA request, we will contact you with a time estimate and a request to extend. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the Chicago FOIA Center.

title: Records Request

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP# 25-19

Title/Description: School Bus Stop-Arm Camera Program

Issuing Entity: Town of Hamden

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

The number of buses installed and number of tickets issued from the beginning of the contract for the school bus program.  

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 


Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: IPRA Request
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The responsive records to the attached request have been completed.  Due to the large file, these records cannot be sent through email.  We will need to download the records onto a thumb drive for a charge of $10.00.  We have 2 options for this process:

Option 1: We download the records onto a thumb drive and have it ready for pick-up here at our office at a scheduled time you choose.  Cost: $10.00

Option 2: We download the records onto a thumb drive and send it through the mail directly to your office.  Before we proceed with this option, we will need a check sent to us for the thumb drive and return postage. Cost: $22.00 ($10.00 for thumb drive & $12.00 for return postage) Address for check to be sent: 660 Main St. NW Los Lunas, NM 87031 (Address check to The Village of Los Lunas)  You can address the envelope to The Village of Los Lunas (Attn: Mykayla Moody)

Let me know what option works best for you, and we will begin the process as soon as possible.


Mykayla Moody 😊
Village of Los Lunas
Administrative Assistant
Phone: 505-839-3840
E-Mail: moodym@loslunasnm.gov

title: MD mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: MD mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Internal evaluation or pilot assessment reports that summarize performance, adoption, risks, or “lessons learned” from Maryland’s mDL program (Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, Samsung Wallet, Maryland Mobile ID for Business) between January 1, 2023 and the present.

Agreements, contracts, or memoranda of understanding with vendors or partners, including but not limited to Apple, Google, Samsung, IDEMIA, AAMVA, or other providers.

Interagency or interstate coordination documents, including but not limited to MOUs or correspondence with AAMVA or other states regarding mDL interoperability, standards development, or airport integration at Baltimore/Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport (BWI).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: Your request has been submitted – Case No: PR-CN00005666
communications.communication:
MDOT MVA Public Records 
   
    
  
Hi Rebecca Williams, 
**This is an automated response that your request has been received. PIA requests will receive a response within 10 business days. Subpoena requests will generally receive a response two weeks prior to the trial or return date you provide.
 Please do NOT respond to this email as the inbox does not accept replies.** 
We look forward to serving you! MDOT MVA has a robust menu of online services that can be completed from your phone, tablet or computer. (https://mva.maryland.gov/online-services/Pages/default.aspx) Create a myMVA profile to take advantage of all that our online services have to offer! (https://mymva.maryland.gov/go/web/createmymvaprofile) 
For those customers that require an in-person visit to MDOT MVA, our offices are open by appointment Monday through Saturday (times vary, see our locations page). Make an appointment (https://mva.maryland.gov/locations/Pages/default.aspx) here. Customers that prefer to use our business kiosks and those that are making a tag return can walk-in to complete those services. VEIP tests are also available without an appointment. (https://license.mva.maryland.gov/CheckList/default.aspx) 
Thank you again for contacting the MDOT MVA. We appreciate hearing from you. 
- TEAM MDOT MVA 
 Maryland
 now features 511 traveler information! 
Visit: https://chart.maryland.gov/ (https://chart.maryland.gov/)  Please consider the environment before printing this email LEGAL DISCLAIMER - The information contained in this communication (including any attachments) may be confidential and legally privileged. This email may not serve as a
 contractual agreement unless explicit written agreement for this purpose has been made. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any dissemination, distribution, or copying of this communication or any of its contents is strictly
 prohibited. If you have received this communication in error, please re-send this communication to the sender indicating that it was received in error and delete the original message and any copy of it from your computer system.

title: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any memos, emails, and/or policies since January 20, 2025, related to the effort between ATF, DEA, FBI, US Marshals, and HSI to locate and interview at-risk unaccompanied alien children who entered the United States over the past four years.

- Any memos, reports, and/or emails drafted since January 20, 2025 quantifying the number and/or percentage of children identified as abused, trafficked, neglected, or exploited through Unaccompanied Alien Children Operations and/or other HSI wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children. 

- All trainings, memos, and/or policies used since January 20, 2025 about communicating with children and/or using trauma-informed interviewing skills provided to agents completing wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lara S Nochomovitz

title: H. Bynam - FOIA US Army Vietnam war

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: H. Bynam - FOIA US Army Vietnam war
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) concerning Holland E. Bynam
Name of Veteran: Holland Bynam
Branch: United States Army
DOB: July 26, 1935
Dates of Service: 1954 - 1984

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, decorations, Badges, campaigns, assignments, Vietnam War service dates
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. request copy of Silver Star Medal award citations 
4. Military Education/ training courses, military schools
5. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citations
6. request copy of Army commendation award citations
7 request copy of legion of merit award citation
8. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form
9. date of discharge
10: Overseas service date
I request copies of paperwork in the file showing this information

First name: Holland
Middle initial: E
Last name: Bynam
date of birth - 07/26/1935
Rank - Colonel
https://olg52.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/COL-H.E.-Bynam-1.jpg

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the Navy's Personnel Files (OMPF) for a former service member named Nicholas Joseph LaLota (US Navy, 2000 - 2008), DOB: 06/23/1978.

The public interest in Nicholas Joseph LaLota, who is running for political office this year in New York (https://lalota.house.gov/), should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist.

Please be sure to review and release any segregable information, even if certain documents or portions of documents are withheld.

PLEASE NOTE: I am not a next of kin of this servicemember, and thus do not know LaLota's Social Security Number. Because of this, I am ONLY requesting records releasable under the Freedom of Information Act. Please process my request for this servicemember's OMPF and other records under the Freedom of Information Act’s relevant provisions. I am not seeking any records that would be releasable under the Privacy Act.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. Furthermore, I am a member of the news media and this request is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin
A fix is required to perfect the request.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
Hello,

I would like to confirm that this response from the NPRC means that the search for this service member's personnel file was unsuccessful with the information I supplied: name, date of birth, dates of service and branch of military. The only information that leaves from the list provided is the service member's social security. Thus, I would like to further confirm that the search was unsuccessful using all the information I supplied and can only be completed with this service member's social security number. Is that correct?

Best,
Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicholas Joseph LaLota Service Record
communications.communication:
To whom it may concern,

This is an appeal under the Freedom of Information Act. My request was sent to the National Personnel Records Center on May 31, 2024, and I received a response on June 26, 2024. I requested a copy of the Navy's Personnel Files (OMPF) for a former service member named Nicholas Joseph LaLota. 

The response that I received on June 26, 2024, stated that the Center was not able to locate the records I requested and asked that I provide as many of the following as possible: the veteran's complete name, date and place of birth, service number, social security number, branch of service, approximate dates of service, place of enlistment and place of separation. I had already provided the officer's full name, branch, dates of service and date of birth. 

In my request, I stated "PLEASE NOTE: I am not a next of kin of this servicemember, and thus do not know LaLota's Social Security Number. Because of this, I am ONLY requesting records releasable under the Freedom of Information Act. Please process my request for this servicemember's OMPF and other records under the Freedom of Information Act’s relevant provisions. I am not seeking any records that would be releasable under the Privacy Act."

On June 26, 2024, I called the NPRC Customer Service Line to speak with a representative about what information I hadn't supplied and what information would be most critical for retrieving the records that I requested. The first representative I spoke with told me that she could see my request was unsuccessful and that I would need to provide the service member's social security number. I told her that I am requesting public records on a public official, and I am not next-of-kin, so I do not have his social security number. She said his social security number would be the only way to retrieve the documents. I asked her whether there was anything else she could tell me about my request and the attempted search. She said no. I hung up and then called back again to see if a different representative would be able to give me more context. But again, the representative told me that the Center couldn't tell me anything more than that I needed the service member's social security number. 

On July 3, 2024, I responded to my request by fax and asked the Center to confirm whether the information I already supplied was not enough and whether this means they require a social security number. As of August 14, 2024, I have not received a reply. 

Only July 15, I emailed the Center's FOIA outreach manager, Lisa M. Sandor. Sandor and I's email communication is attached to this appeal as a PDF. Sandor reiterated what I heard from employees on the phone: that she couldn't tell me more about the search associated with request, beyond that I would just need a social security number. 

I believe I have demonstrated due diligence and attempted to work with the NRPC to refine or clarify my search for records, but have received inadequate response from NRPC employees. I have not received a clear explanation as to why a social security number is needed in this instance and whether there are any other ways that I can improve my request to result in a successful search without the service member's social security number. I am concerned that if public records on a public official are only public if the requester has access to that public official's social security number, those records are effectively not public to begin with.  

Furthermore, there is strong public interest in Nicholas Joseph LaLota, who is running for political office this year in New York  (https://lalota.house.gov/) and that interest should favor disclosure of many of these records, particularly in relation to any disciplinary records, should those records exist. The right to know and verify a politician's claims about military service is an essential tool for journalists. If these records require a service member's social security number, the records are inaccessible to the media. 

If you need to discuss this request, I can be reached at (267) 314-7952. Thank you for your consideration of this appeal.

My best,
Dillon Bergin

title: Palantir

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Palantir
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting records related to the department's use of Palantir's Gotham software
Contracts with Palantir and any contractor that manages and assists with running the program
MoUs with other agencies, including data sharing agreements. 
MoUs with other technology companies, which include: ALPR's, surveillance cameras, social media surveillance, facial recognition 
Documentation of data sources from law enforcement databases, for example: CLETS, LACRIS, CLEAR,  etc

Search 2020 to today's date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Palantir

title: E & A Occupancy + Fire Sprinkler Information (Eagle Pass City Council)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: E & A Occupancy + Fire Sprinkler Information (Eagle Pass City Council)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request an electronic, parcel-level extract containing current Group E (Educational) and Group A (Assembly) occupancies and associated fire-sprinkler information for all parcels within the jurisdiction: 

Please provide one CSV with the following columns (your native field names are fine):
- Parcel identifier (APN / PIN / BBL)
• Site address (street #, street, city, ZIP)
• Latitude & longitude
• Internal building / permit ID
• Year built
• Floor area (sq ft)
• Number of stories
• Current IBC occupancy code (E, A-1…A-5)
• Occupancy description
• Fire-sprinkler present (Yes / No / Unknown)
• Date of last fire-sprinkler inspection (if tracked)
• Owner name & mailing address (if stored)

If any field is not maintained, please note that rather than withholding the entire record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Cavise

title: Response to Medical Emergencies - Connecticut Department of Correction

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Response to Medical Emergencies - Connecticut Department of Correction
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All written policies and procedures of the Connecticut Department of Correction governing responses to medical emergencies, including policies related to the transport of incarcerated individuals to community hospitals or other external medical facilities.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophie Choong

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Marines support for NCIS, Chopped, XVII Carvings, The Ultimate Fighter, To What Remains, and The Price Is Right

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for Food Network reality TV series "Chopped"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the Food Network reality TV series "Chopped." According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Chopped," including all episodes with which the Marines were involved. Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Chopped," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Information on Flock Saftey and AI-assisted Cameras
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The location of all Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras installed in the last 90 days (please share locations as map coordinates or a street address with nearest cross-street)
- The number of Flock Safety or other AI-assisted cameras planned to be installed and the location where JPD plans to put them (please share locations as map coordinates or a street address with nearest cross-street)
- All email communications with Immigration and Customs Enforcement regarding data sharing from Flock Safety cameras
- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers (ALPRs) over the last 30 days
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety automated license plate readers
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- All agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen

title: Deaths-in-custody

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Deaths-in-custody
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The database or records of all Illinois law enforcement agencies deaths-in-custody up to the date this request is ultimately fulfilled, including all deaths-in-custody reported pursuant both Illinois state legislation (730 ILCS 210/3-5) and federal legislation (34 U.S.C. § 60105), as well as all data dictionaries, database scheme documentation or other documentation on the collection and organizatin of the data. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: 2026 Ohio (Office of the Attorney General - Georgia)

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: 2026 Ohio (Office of the Attorney General - Georgia)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Ohio Freedom of Information Law and the new 2026 Ohio Sunshine Laws Manual I hereby request the following records:
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code, I hereby request the following records:  unofficial copies of any documents of complaints and actions taken (any info allowable by law) filed with the Ohio Charitable Law and the Ohio Secretary of State regarding :


Congregation Sholom
DBA: Temple Sholom of Cincinnati (doing business as that has lapsed)
10828 Kenwood Rd. Cincinnati, OH 45242
EIN 31-0584318


Central Conference of American Rabbis - OH Registration #26991
EIN: 13-1769747
NY State Registration Number - 05-99-38
Recently changed address:
183 Madison Ave #1504
New York, NY 10016
Address listed with Charities Bureau:
355 LEXINGTON AVE 8th Floor, null
NEW YORK , NY 10017

Ohio Subsidiary
Ohio Entity ID 26991
1160 Dublin Road, Suite 400
Columbus OH 43215
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code & New York Public Officers Law § 87(2), I request access to and copies of all non-exempt public records in the possession, custody, or control of the Ohio Attorney General or any other public office relating to the Central Conference of American Rabbis & Temple Sholom (Cincinnati, Ohio), including but not limited to records connected to any complaints, investigations, or actions taken under Ohio Charitable Law.
If any portion of the requested records is withheld, I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions be provided, with redactions. 
This request specifically includes, and is not limited to:
All copies of any and all organizational and governing documents submitted to, filed with, or otherwise in the possession of any public office relating to Congregation Sholom (Temple Sholom of Cincinnati) and the Central Conference of American Rabbis, including but not limited to articles of incorporation or association, bylaws, constitutions, amendments, and any other governance or structural documents.
All complaints submitted against Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents, submitted to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits.
All documents, correspondence, and materials submitted by Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits
All communications between Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis  (or its representatives) and any public office, including emails, letters, requests for information, follow-up communications, and meeting summaries.
All documents, records, or materials that were reviewed, relied upon, cited, or considered by any public office in connection with complaints, inquiries, or investigations involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis regardless of whether such materials originated from Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis or third parties.
All records created by any public office relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including but not limited to investigative summaries, factual findings, memoranda, recommendations, determinations, closure letters, or case status communications.
Any financial, contractual, or funding-related records involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis that were submitted to or reviewed by a public office, including any materials referencing grants, loans, or other funding (including but not limited to COVID-related funding) if such materials were part of any complaint, review, or investigation.
All complaints and supporting documentation submitted by third parties relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including any attachments or exhibits.
All final or closed-case materials, sanctions, settlements, assurance of discontinuance agreements, administrative subpoenas, court filings, court orders, closure letters and determination summaries.
Any Foia Requests made to the Attorney General for information on Temple Sholom or Central Conference of American Rabbis, the date of the request, the name of the person making the request, and the responsive documents provided.
I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am a complainant and a writer for the Times of Israel and I am requesting this information in that editorial capacity.  The Times of Israel is widely recognized for its reporting, publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance.  The information may also be used in a documentary that is currently being filed.  You may view my articles at the address listed:  https://blogs.timesofisrael.com/author/julie-guilfoyle/
If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.  If there is a large cost, then as an alternative, I request for the records to be made available for inspection.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request.
Sincerely,
Julie Guilfoyle

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julie Guilfoyle

title: Department of Defense support for TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska" (2020-2023). Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Ice Airport Alaska," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Ice Airport Alaska" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo
